behavior_summary: This script provides the 'U8P_MAGARMR2::look' / examine handler for a magic armor object. When the player inspects the object the process emits a single short descriptive bark "magic armor " and then suspends/ends the U8P_MAGARMR2::look process.

The object carries an item property armourClass = 6 (recorded in metadata) indicating it represents armor with AC 6; this is an associated data property rather than manipulated at runtime by this script.
No menus, conditions, global flags, or external calls are present; the routine is a simple display-of-text + suspend sequence.
notable_control_flow: U8P_MAGARMR2::look is a process-form handler (process type 0x0208) triggered by the game's U8P_MAGARMR2::look/examine action.
Control flow is linear: display Bark("magic armor ") then suspend the process (suspend inferred from Unknown node).
There are no conditional branches, loops, or external interactions in this script.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the bark text "magic armor " intentional (formatting) or an extraction artifact?
Is armourClass = 6 relied on elsewhere by gameplay code, or is it purely descriptive metadata for inventories/equipment systems?
Are there any alternate entry events (e.g., fast-area enter) that could invoke this process besides the canonical 'U8P_MAGARMR2::look' action?

main_function.name: U8P_MAGARMR2::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the player's U8P_MAGARMR2::look/examine action for the magic armor by showing a short descriptive text and then suspending the process.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player uses the 'U8P_MAGARMR2::look' (examine) command on this object (inferred from process type 0x0208 and function classification).

function_map.name: U8P_MAGARMR2::look
function_map.role: Present short description (bark) for the magic armor and suspend the U8P_MAGARMR2::look process
